seo.title: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC AcroForm XFA ANSI-Unicode Confusion Information Leak Vulnerability - HackSys Inc
seo.description: Adobe Acrobat Reader DC has an information leak vulnerability in the AcroForm module due to ANSI-Unicode confusion, allowing remote attackers to disclose sensitive data. User interaction, like visiting a malicious page or opening a malicious file, is needed for exploitation. This flaw can be combined with other vulnerabilities for arbitrary code execution. (CVE-2021-45067)

pageProps.content:
## Vulnerability

Adobe Acrobat Reader DC AcroForm XFA ANSI-Unicode Confusion Information Leak Vulnerability

## Vulnerability Description

This vulnerability allows remote attackers to disclose sensitive information on affected installations of Adobe Acrobat Reader DC. User interaction is required to exploit this vulnerability in that the target must visit a malicious page or open a malicious file.

The specific flaw exists within the handling of Fields within AcroForm module. By performing actions in JavaScript, an attacker can trigger a read past the end of an allocated object. An attacker can leverage this in conjunction with other vulnerabilities to execute arbitrary code in the context of the current process.

## CVE ID

CVE-2021-45067

## Vendor

[Adobe](https://www.adobe.com/)

## Products

- Acrobat Reader DC - 2021.007.20095 and earlier

## Disclosure Timeline

- 05 October 2021 - Vulnerability reported to ZDI
- 04 November 2021 - ZDI rejected the vulnerability
- 10 November 2021 - Vulnerability reported to vendor
- 11 January 2022 - Coordinated public release of advisory

## Credits

Ashfaq Ansari and Krishnakant Patil - HackSys Inc

## Vendor Advisory

<https://helpx.adobe.com/security/products/acrobat/apsb22-01.html>

## Demo Video

<iframe width="100%" height="450" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/2QNmhwN_I4w" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>
